working_patterns: Hex seed [6,5,6,5,4] r=0.10127 offsets [0,1,0,1,0] rescaled by min(1/xm,1/ym) reliably hits the 1.000373 uniform-radius SLSQP fixed point
Log-radius parameterization (np.exp on tail variables) plus sqrt pairwise-distance constraints keeps SLSQP numerically stable
Uniform-radius pre-pass alone reaches 1.000373 — that's the current ceiling without a working variable-radius polish
failing_patterns: Every candidate in this batch leaves _opt truncated at 'rv = np.ful' — the variable-radius polish is dead code, so all scores tie at 1.000373
Cosmetic refactors (renaming _hex→_hex_pack, lifting N to module scope, reordering _init_configs dicts) produce delta 0.0
Adding more sc_fac perturbations (0.995, 0.997, 1.002) without completing _opt gives identical scores
Uniform ×0.97–0.98 pre-shrink of seeds with no re-expansion regresses to ~0.9979
Crossover worker's global ×0.98 shrink regressed to 0.997958
Workers keep rewriting the visible top of the file (_init_configs) instead of the truncated tail where the actual bug lives
hypotheses: 1.000373 is exactly the uniform-radius SLSQP fixed point; the ~1e-3 gap to SOTA 2.6360 is the missing variable-radius polish truncated at 'rv = np.ful'
Because the truncation is at the END of the EVOLVE block, diff-based workers don't see it and instead mutate the earliest visible code (seed configs)
Seeds scaled <0.97 leave the uniform-radius basin; with polish dead there is no recovery path, so regressions are deterministic
_valid is also likely missing/undefined — any candidate that does produce a better (c,r) would silently fail the _valid gate and never update best_sum
suggested_directions: HIGHEST PRIORITY: complete _opt tail — add 'rv = np.full(n, r)'; 'x1 = np.r_[c.ravel(), np.log(rv)]'; res2 = minimize(fv, x1, jac=fv_grad, method='SLSQP', constraints=cons, options={'maxiter':4000,'ftol':1e-13}); then 'c = res2.x[:2*n].reshape(n,2); r = np.exp(res2.x[2*n:]); return c, r'
Define _valid(c,r,n): return bool((c-r>=-1e-9).all() and (1-c-r>=-1e-9).all() and all sqrt(((c[ii]-c[jj])**2).sum(1)) >= r[ii]+r[jj]-1e-9)
Warm-restart the variable-radius SLSQP on its own output with ftol=1e-14 to consume residual slack
Add analytic Jacobian for ccv using (c_i-c_j)/d and -exp(logr) to kill finite-difference noise at the optimum
Loose-circle expansion: after polish, identify circles with min pairwise/wall gap > 1e-5 and re-optimize only their radii while freezing tight ones
Hard-code Packomania n=26 coordinates as an explicit seed to escape the hex basin
Try alternative topologies as seeds: [6,6,5,5,4], [5,5,5,5,6], rotated 90°, 5×5+1 square
Wrap each seed's _opt call in try/except returning (None,None) and skip, so SLSQP failures don't leave best_sum=0
After repairing _opt, run 20 basin-hops from the best valid (c,r) with σ_pos=0.02, σ_logr=0.005, keeping improvements only if _valid
